summary:
The claim "Zika is not transmitted from a pregnant woman to her baby" is inaccurate; studies show transmission between these individuals occurs and thus makes Statement C untrue about the Zika virus.
The other statements align accurately with known characteristics of the Zika virus based on current medical understanding. Therefore, only one statement misrepresents its nature or spread among populations affected by infection. To clarify which specific detail was misrepresented requires further examination but here focuses primarily on disproving non-transmission during pregnancy as erroneous information pertinent directly capable transmitting diseases through saliva secretions into human skin tissues upon biting host individual thereby facilitating viral entry leading ultimately towards establishment inside body where
